definitions.description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.

builderHint.searchHint: Wire model/memory/tools/outputParser via the SDK `subnodes` config object using factory functions (`languageModel()`, `memory()`, `tool()`, `outputParser()`). Inside subnodes, reference upstream data with `nodeJson(triggerNode, 'path')`, not `$json` — subnodes do not share the main predecessor's item context.

relatedNodes.relationHint: Use to combine multiple items together before the agent
Attach for structured output; reference fields as $json.output.fieldName for use in subsequent nodes (conditions, storing data)
For multi-agent systems using orchestrator pattern
Required for conversational workflows - connect memory to every agent that needs to recall previous messages in the conversation

extraTypeDefContent.content:
<patterns>
<pattern title="Agent with model, memory, structured output parser">
const chatTrigger = trigger({
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger',
  version: 1.3,
  config: {
    name: 'Chat Trigger',
    parameters: { public: false },
    output: [{ sessionId: 'chat-session-id', chatInput: 'Hello' }]
  }
});

const model = languageModel({
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi',
  version: 1.3,
  config: {
    name: 'OpenAI Chat Model',
    parameters: { model: { __rl: true, mode: 'list', value: 'gpt-5.4' } },
    credentials: { openAiApi: { id: 'credId', name: 'OpenAI account' } }
  }
});

const parser = outputParser({
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.outputParserStructured',
  version: 1.3,
  config: {
    name: 'Output Parser',
    parameters: {
      schemaType: 'fromJson',
      jsonSchemaExample: '{ "score": 75, "tier": "hot" }'
    }
  }
});

const memoryNode = memory({
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow',
  version: 1.3,
  config: {
    name: 'Conversation Memory',
    parameters: {
      sessionIdType: 'customKey',
      sessionKey: nodeJson(chatTrigger, 'sessionId'),
      contextWindowLength: 10
    }
  }
});

const agent = node({
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent',
  version: 3.1,
  config: {
    name: 'AI Agent',
    parameters: {
      promptType: 'define',
      text: expr('{{ $json.prompt }}'),
      hasOutputParser: true,
      options: { systemMessage: 'You are an expert...' }
    },
    subnodes: { model, memory: memoryNode, outputParser: parser }
  }
});
</pattern>
</patterns>

properties.displayName: Tip: Get a feel for agents with our quick tutorial or see an example of how this node works
properties.name: aiAgentStarterCallout

options.name: Connected Chat Trigger Node
options.description: Looks for an input field called 'chatInput' that is coming from a directly connected Chat Trigger

options.name: Connected Guardrails Node
options.description: Looks for an input field called 'guardrailsInput' that is coming from a directly connected Guardrails Node

options.name: Define below
options.description: Use an expression to reference data in previous nodes or enter static text

options.name: Connected Chat Trigger Node
options.description: Looks for an input field called 'chatInput' that is coming from a directly connected Chat Trigger

options.name: Define below
options.description: Use an expression to reference data in previous nodes or enter static text

builderHint.propertyHint: Use 'auto' when following a chat trigger, 'define' when custom prompt needed

properties.name: text
properties.placeholder: e.g. Hello, how can you help me?

builderHint.propertyHint: Use expressions to include dynamic data from previous nodes (e.g., expr('{{ $json.input }}')). Static text prompts ignore incoming data.

Set to `true` when you need structured JSON output. The agent then requires an `outputParser` entry in its `subnodes` config (typically an `outputParserStructured` node defined via the `outputParser({...})` SDK factory). With `hasOutputParser: false` the agent returns a plain string in `$json.output`.

properties.displayName: Connect an output parser on the canvas to specify the output format you require
properties.name: notice

properties.displayName: Connect an additional language model on the canvas to use it as a fallback if the main model fails
properties.name: fallbackNotice

options.name: systemMessage
options.description: The message that will be sent to the agent before the conversation starts

builderHint.propertyHint: Must include: agent's purpose, exact names of connected tools, and response instructions

options.name: maxIterations
options.description: The maximum number of iterations the agent will run before stopping

options.name: returnIntermediateSteps
options.description: Whether or not the output should include intermediate steps the agent took

options.name: passthroughBinaryImages
options.description: Whether or not binary images should be automatically passed through to the agent as image type messages

options.name: passthroughBinaryPdfs
options.description: Whether or not binary PDF documents should be automatically passed through to the agent. Useful for models that natively support PDF input (e.g. Google Gemini).

options.name: tracingMetadata
options.description: Custom metadata added to tracing events

options.name: autoSaveHighlightedData
options.description: Whether to automatically save highlighted data. This data can then be used to filter executions in the Executions view. Available on Pro and Enterprise plans in n8n Cloud, and on Enterprise or registered Community Edition for self-hosted. Defaults to true.

options.name: enableStreaming
options.description: Whether this agent will stream the response in real-time as it generates text

options.name: batching
options.description: Batch processing options for rate limiting

options.name: batchSize
options.description: How many items to process in parallel. This is useful for rate limiting, but might impact the log output ordering.

options.name: delayBetweenBatches
options.description: Delay in milliseconds between batches. This is useful for rate limiting.

options.displayName: Max Tokens To Read From Memory
options.name: maxTokensFromMemory
options.description: The maximum number of tokens to read from the chat memory history. Set to 0 to read all history.

hints.message: You are using streaming responses. Make sure to set the response mode to "Streaming Response" on the connected trigger node.

properties.displayName: Tip: Get a feel for agents with our quick tutorial or see an example of how this node works
properties.name: aiAgentStarterCallout

properties.displayName: This node is using Agent that has been deprecated. Please switch to using 'Tools Agent' instead.
properties.name: deprecated

options.name: Conversational Agent
options.description: Describes tools in the system prompt and parses JSON responses for tool calls. More flexible but potentially less reliable than the Tools Agent. Suitable for simpler interactions or with models not supporting structured schemas.

options.name: OpenAI Functions Agent
options.description: Leverages OpenAI's function calling capabilities to precisely select and execute tools. Excellent for tasks requiring structured outputs when working with OpenAI models.

options.name: Plan and Execute Agent
options.description: Creates a high-level plan for complex tasks and then executes each step. Suitable for multi-stage problems or when a strategic approach is needed.

options.name: ReAct Agent
options.description: Combines reasoning and action in an iterative process. Effective for tasks that require careful analysis and step-by-step problem-solving.

options.name: SQL Agent
options.description: Specializes in interacting with SQL databases. Ideal for data analysis tasks, generating queries, or extracting insights from structured data.

options.name: Tools Agent
options.description: Utilizes structured tool schemas for precise and reliable tool selection and execution. Recommended for complex tasks requiring accurate and consistent tool usage, but only usable with models that support tool calling.

options.name: Conversational Agent
options.description: Describes tools in the system prompt and parses JSON responses for tool calls. More flexible but potentially less reliable than the Tools Agent. Suitable for simpler interactions or with models not supporting structured schemas.

options.name: systemMessage
options.default:
Assistant is a large language model trained by OpenAI.

Assistant is designed to be able to assist with a wide range of tasks, from answering simple questions to providing in-depth explanations and discussions on a wide range of topics. As a language model, Assistant is able to generate human-like text based on the input it receives, allowing it to engage in natural-sounding conversations and provide responses that are coherent and relevant to the topic at hand.

Assistant is constantly learning and improving, and its capabilities are constantly evolving. It is able to process and understand large amounts of text, and can use this knowledge to provide accurate and informative responses to a wide range of questions. Additionally, Assistant is able to generate its own text based on the input it receives, allowing it to engage in discussions and provide explanations and descriptions on a wide range of topics.

Overall, Assistant is a powerful system that can help with a wide range of tasks and provide valuable insights and information on a wide range of topics. Whether you need help with a specific question or just want to have a conversation about a particular topic, Assistant is here to assist.
options.description: The message that will be sent to the agent before the conversation starts

options.description: The maximum number of iterations the agent will run before stopping

options.description: Whether or not the output should include intermediate steps the agent took

options.name: prefix
options.default: Answer the following questions as best you can. You have access to the following tools:
options.description: String to put before the list of tools

options.name: suffixChat
options.default: Begin! Reminder to always use the exact characters `Final Answer` when responding.
options.description: String to put after the list of tools that will be used if chat model is used

options.name: suffix
options.description: String to put after the list of tools that will be used if regular model is used

options.description: The maximum number of iterations the agent will run before stopping

options.name: returnIntermediateSteps
options.description: Whether or not the output should include intermediate steps the agent took

options.description: Connect to a MySQL database

properties.name: text

builderHint.propertyHint: Use expressions to include dynamic data from previous nodes (e.g., expr('{{ $json.input }}')). Static text prompts ignore incoming data.

options.name: ignoredTables
options.description: Comma-separated list of tables to ignore from the database. If empty, no tables are ignored.

options.name: includedSampleRows
options.description: Number of sample rows to include in the prompt to the agent. It helps the agent to understand the schema of the database but it also increases the amount of tokens used.

options.name: includedTables
options.description: Comma-separated list of tables to include in the database. If empty, all tables are included.

options.name: prefixPrompt
options.default:
You are an agent designed to interact with an SQL database.
Given an input question, create a syntactically correct {dialect} query to run, then look at the results of the query and return the answer.
Unless the user specifies a specific number of examples they wish to obtain, always limit your query to at most {top_k} results using the LIMIT clause.
You can order the results by a relevant column to return the most interesting examples in the database.
Never query for all the columns from a specific table, only ask for a the few relevant columns given the question.
You have access to tools for interacting with the database.
Only use the below tools. Only use the information returned by the below tools to construct your final answer.
You MUST double check your query before executing it. If you get an error while executing a query, rewrite the query and try again.

DO NOT make any DML statements (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP etc.) to the database.

If the question does not seem related to the database, just return "I don't know" as the answer.
options.description: Prefix prompt to use for the agent

options.name: suffixPrompt
options.description: Suffix prompt to use for the agent

options.name: topK
options.description: The maximum number of results to return

options.name: tracingMetadata
options.description: Custom metadata added to tracing events

options.name: humanMessageTemplate
options.description: The message that will be sent to the agent during each step execution

options.name: tracingMetadata
options.description: Custom metadata added to tracing events
